reasoning: The fetch_ts tool shows APA's stock recently crashed from around ~$20 dropping all the way to $14.30 on April 11. The linear model uses an extrapolation of the short term trend line (which implies that the stock is currently undershooting its rolling trend) and estimates P(>14.3) at 2025-04-20 to be 0.54. The combined estimate in the prompt was slightly higher (0.60) due to a single season sample, but the strict instructions remind us that weekly stock moves are largely unpredictable and we should stay close to 0.5. Therefore, a slight mean-reversion pull gives 0.54 for the immediate 1-week horizon. As forecast horizons increase out to 1 month, 3 months, and 6 months, variance scales up dramatically. Following the specific instructions that uncertainty should increase with forecast horizon and probabilities for distant dates should be closer to 0.5, I gradually regress the prediction toward the 0.5 base rate for weekly/monthly stock returns.
